testModifications.reasoning: No test files were modified by the implementer. All tests were created by the test-writer in commit a81434f and all 45 GitHub provider integration tests pass without modification.

criteria.criterion: GitHubProvider.fetchIssues() calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues with state=open, sort=created, direction=asc and filters out entries where pull_request field is present
criteria.evidence: Implementation at github.provider.ts:33-58. Tests verify correct URL and parameters via 'should call GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues with correct parameters' and pull request filtering via 'should filter out entries where pull_request field is present'

criteria.criterion: GitHubProvider.fetchIssues(since) passes ISO 8601 since parameter to the GitHub issues endpoint
criteria.evidence: Implementation at github.provider.ts:40-42. Test verifies via 'should pass ISO 8601 since parameter when provided' and 'should convert since Date to ISO string correctly'

criteria.criterion: GitHubProvider.fetchIssue(issueNumber) calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issueNumber} and returns a mapped VcsIssue
criteria.evidence: Implementation at github.provider.ts:60-78. Tests verify correct endpoint via 'should call GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issueNumber}' and mapping via 'should return mapped VcsIssue'

criteria.criterion: GitHubProvider.fetchIssue(nonexistent) throws NotFoundAppException when GitHub returns 404
criteria.evidence: Implementation at github.provider.ts:71-75. Tests verify via 'should throw NotFoundAppException when GitHub returns 404' and 'should throw NotFoundAppException for 404 responses'

criteria.criterion: GitHubProvider.testConnection() returns { ok: true } when token has repo read access
criteria.evidence: Implementation at github.provider.ts:80-95. Tests verify via 'should return { ok: true } when successful' and 'should have ok property set to true'

criteria.criterion: GitHubProvider.testConnection() returns { ok: false, error: 'message' } when the token is invalid or repo is not found
criteria.evidence: Implementation at github.provider.ts:91-94. Tests verify via 'should return { ok: false, error: message } on error', 'should handle 404 (repo not found) by returning ok: false', and 'should handle 401 (invalid token) by returning ok: false'

criteria.criterion: All HTTP requests include Authorization: Bearer {token} header
criteria.evidence: Implementation includes Authorization header in fetchIssues (line 48-49), fetchIssue (line 65-67), and testConnection (line 85-87). Tests verify via multiple 'should include Authorization Bearer header with token' tests

criteria.criterion: GitHub API responses are mapped to VcsIssue shape (number, title, body, authorLogin, url, labels, createdAt)
criteria.evidence: Implementation at github.provider.ts:97-107. Maps all required fields correctly. Tests verify mapping via 'should return mapped VcsIssue array' and 'should return mapped VcsIssue'. Date parsing verified via 'should parse ISO 8601 created_at correctly'. Label mapping verified via 'should map multiple labels correctly'

details.codeStyle: Implementation follows TypeScript strict mode and project conventions
details.errorHandling: Proper error handling with NotFoundAppException for 404s and error message extraction in testConnection
details.typeSafety: No 'any' types in production code, proper typing of GitHubIssueResponse interface
details.interfaces: Correctly implements IVcsProvider interface with all required methods
details.responseMappings: All GitHub API fields correctly mapped to VcsIssue type with proper date parsing
details.edgeCases: Handles null body, empty issues list, multiple labels, and pull_request field variations correctly
details.linting: No ESLint warnings or errors (verified with npm run lint)
details.typeChecking: No TypeScript type errors (verified with npm run type-check)

reasoning: The GitHubProvider implementation is complete and correct. All 45 story-scoped integration tests pass along with all 806 project-wide tests. The implementation satisfies all 8 acceptance criteria with clean code that properly handles authentication, error cases, and response mapping. No modifications or fixes were needed. The code follows project conventions, passes linting, and has no type errors. The test coverage is comprehensive, verifying not only happy paths but also edge cases and error handling.
